line: GROVE_RESULT: success - implemented TUI guard indicator with live violation count and interactive guard editing

event.summary: success - implemented TUI guard indicator with live violation count and interactive guard editing

line: - Guard violation totals needed to be recomputed against the active guard set when loading snapshots; persisting only the latest violating file was not enough for the TUI acceptance criteria.

items: Guard violation totals needed to be recomputed against the active guard set when loading snapshots; persisting only the latest violating file was not enough for the TUI acceptance criteria.

line: - The workspace still has an unrelated `tokio::select!` compile issue in `crates/saw-daemon/src/jsonl_tail.rs`, so validation had to rely on `cargo test -p saw --bin saw` and `cargo test -p saw-core`.

items: The workspace still has an unrelated `tokio::select!` compile issue in `crates/saw-daemon/src/jsonl_tail.rs`, so validation had to rely on `cargo test -p saw --bin saw` and `cargo test -p saw-core`.

line: - Added `scope_violation_count` to `AgentState` and incremented it from both write-tool and file-watcher events so the guard bar can show accurate totals.

items: Added `scope_violation_count` to `AgentState` and incremented it from both write-tool and file-watcher events so the guard bar can show accurate totals.

line: - Changed `load_state`/`collect_snapshot` to apply normalized guards before replaying session events, ensuring violation counts are calculated for the currently selected guard path(s), including after pressing `g`.

items: Changed `load_state`/`collect_snapshot` to apply normalized guards before replaying session events, ensuring violation counts are calculated for the currently selected guard path(s), including after pressing `g`.

line: - Implemented the “hover/select” detail as a dedicated second line in the guard panel plus an edit dialog that also shows the last violated file while editing, since ratatui doesn’t provide mouse hover semantics in the current TUI.

items: Implemented the “hover/select” detail as a dedicated second line in the guard panel plus an edit dialog that also shows the last violated file while editing, since ratatui doesn’t provide mouse hover semantics in the current TUI.

line: - The TUI accepts comma-separated guard paths in the `g` dialog; it does not yet provide per-path cursor navigation or a richer selector widget.

items: The TUI accepts comma-separated guard paths in the `g` dialog; it does not yet provide per-path cursor navigation or a richer selector widget.

line: - Full-workspace `cargo check`/`cargo build` still fail because of the pre-existing daemon `tokio::select!` issue, not because of this TUI change.

items: Full-workspace `cargo check`/`cargo build` still fail because of the pre-existing daemon `tokio::select!` issue, not because of this TUI change.

line: - Guard bar now renders below metrics with clear good/bad styling.
- Second line shows last violated file.
- `g` opens an input dialog to set/update/clear guard paths.
- Saving the dialog immediately re-evaluates scope violations against the new guard set.
